* feat: universal VFS (/hd, /cdrom), shell history with arrow keys, fix boot.img in ISO

- iso.sh: copy boot.img from host GRUB into isodir/boot/grub/i386-pc/ so
  the installer can read it from the ISO9660 filesystem
- keyboard.h: add KEY_ARROW_UP / KEY_ARROW_DOWN sentinel characters
- keyboard.c: handle PS/2 extended scan code 0xE0 prefix; emit
  KEY_ARROW_UP (0x01) for E0 48 and KEY_ARROW_DOWN (0x02) for E0 50
- vfs.h/vfs.c (new): lightweight VFS routing layer
    - /hd/...    -> FAT32 hard-disk partition
    - /cdrom/... -> ISO9660 CD-ROM (auto-detected on vfs_init)
    - /          -> virtual root (ls shows available mount-points)
  path_normalize() handles ., .., and multiple separators
  vfs_init() probes ATAPI drives for an ISO9660 volume
- shell.c: replace fat32 include with vfs.h; prompt now shows vfs_getcwd()
  (e.g. root@makar /hd/boot~>); add 16-entry command history ring-buffer
  recalled with arrow-up/down; call vfs_init() before REPL loop
- shell_cmds.c: ls/cd/cat/mkdir route through vfs_ls/vfs_cd/vfs_cat/vfs_mkdir;
  mount/umount notify VFS of state changes via vfs_notify_hd_mounted/unmounted
- installer.c: call vfs_notify_hd_unmounted() after fat32_unmount() so the
  shell prompt CWD stays consistent
- shell_help.c: update help text to describe universal VFS paths
- make.config: add vfs.o to KERNEL_ARCH_OBJS

Co-authored-by: Arawn-Davies <19764552+Arawn-Davies@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: include core.img and all GRUB modules in ISO directory tree

iso.sh now uses grub-mkimage to generate a standalone BIOS-bootable
core.img (with biosdisk/part_msdos/fat/normal/multiboot2/linux baked in,
prefix=/boot/grub) and copies it into isodir/boot/grub/i386-pc/ alongside
boot.img and all five .mod files the installer copies to the FAT32 partition.
All GRUB files the installer reads via ISO9660 are now present as regular
files in the ISO directory tree.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/Arawn-Davies/Makar/sessions/242671dc-0293-4a15-979c-0b63892c257e

Co-authored-by: Arawn-Davies <19764552+Arawn-Davies@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: Rock Ridge NM support in ISO9660 driver for long filenames

Add rr_get_name() which reads RRIP NM System Use entries from each
directory record to retrieve the real POSIX filename. dir_find() now
prefers the RR name over the truncated ISO9660 Level-1 8.3 identifier,
fixing resolution of makar.kernel, part_msdos.mod, and multiboot2.mod.
iso9660_ls() also displays RR names. Fixed su_off padding direction
(add 1 when odd, not when even). Added named constants for NM flags.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/Arawn-Davies/Makar/sessions/72a4e4e8-87d5-4217-bd5a-a0085b830d5c

Co-authored-by: Arawn-Davies <19764552+Arawn-Davies@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: FAT32 LFN write support for long filenames

Add lfn_needs_lfn(), make_basis_name(), lfn_checksum(), and
dir_add_with_lfn() to fat32.c. fat32_write_file() and fat32_mkdir()
now call dir_add_with_lfn() which writes VFAT LFN entries before the
8.3 basis-name entry when a filename cannot fit in 8.3 format. This
fixes makar.kernel being written as makar.ker, and part_msdos.mod /
multiboot2.mod being truncated on the installed FAT32 partition. GRUB
can now find the files by their full names and load multiboot2.mod,
allowing 'multiboot2 /boot/makar.kernel' in grub.cfg to succeed.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/Arawn-Davies/Makar/sessions/9058c18f-536c-45c5-ad88-295140f69eeb

Co-authored-by: Arawn-Davies <19764552+Arawn-Davies@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: FAT32 build errors - forward-declare dir_add_entry and move s_lfn_char_off before dir_add_with_lfn

dir_add_with_lfn referenced both dir_add_entry (defined later) and
s_lfn_char_off (declared later at the dir_scan site), causing an
implicit-declaration error, a conflicting-types error, and an
undeclared-identifier error. Fix by:
 - Moving s_lfn_char_off definition to before the LFN helpers block
 - Adding a static forward declaration of dir_add_entry before
   dir_add_with_lfn
 - Removing the now-redundant s_lfn_char_off at the dir_scan site

commit.message:
fix: restrict unsigned binary check to macOS only (#3561)

Linux does not SIGKILL unsigned binaries, so the go-install path
was incorrectly crashing on Linux. Scope the os.Exit(1) guard to
darwin only via runtime.GOOS check.

Update README to clarify that `go install` is Linux-only; macOS
users must use Homebrew or `make build` (which signs the binary).

Fixes https://github.com/gastownhall/gastown/issues/3560

commit.message:
Auto-reconnect to running scans when repo is selected

- Add change listener to repo-select dropdown
- When repo selected, automatically detect running experiments
- If scan in progress, auto-reconnect without confirmation prompt
- Reconnection streams output from running experiment via /scan endpoint
- Server lock file ensures we connect to existing scan, not start new one
- Improves UX on page refresh - users see running scan continue instantly

Flow:
  1. User refreshes page
  2. Selects repo from dropdown
  3. JavaScript detects running_experiment from API
  4. Auto-reconnects and shows live output
  5. No confirmation dialog needed

* staged

* Fix inline mode double-draw by deferring initial LayoutAndDraw

Begin() was calling LayoutAndDraw() directly before the main loop started,
bypassing the IterationImpl deferral logic. In inline mode this caused a
first draw using the default 80x25 fallback size (placing the view at the
wrong row), followed by a second correct draw once the ANSI size monitor
reported the real terminal dimensions.

Changes:
- Skip LayoutAndDraw() in Begin() when AppModel.Inline is active; the
  first render is deferred to IterationImpl until InitialSizeReceived
  is confirmed (or 500ms timeout).
- Add InitialSizeReceived to ISizeMonitor/AnsiSizeMonitor so the main
  loop knows when the real terminal size has arrived.
- Add inline size deferral logic in IterationImpl with Trace
  instrumentation for diagnostics.
- Remove QueryNativeTerminalSize() from AnsiOutput (pure ANSI only).
- Update InlineCLI example: replace Label with ListView + ObservableCollection.
- Add InlineDrawTimingTests (4 trace-based tests proving deferral works).
- Add AnsiSizeMonitor InitialSizeReceived tests.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Position inline view at cursor row via ANSI CPR query

In inline mode, the view was always drawn at the bottom of the terminal
because AnchorEnd() positioned against the full terminal height. When the
cursor was near the top, the view appeared far below the expected position.

Fix: query the cursor position via ANSI DECXCPR (CSI ?6n) at startup and
use the response to:
- Resize Screen.Height to (termHeight - cursorRow) so the view occupies
  only the rows available below the cursor.
- Set InlineRowOffset on AnsiOutput so Screen row 0 maps to the cursor's
  terminal row in SetCursorPositionImpl.

Changes:
- ISizeMonitor: add InitialCursorRow property (default 0)
- AnsiSizeMonitor: send CPR query in Initialize() for inline mode; parse
  DECXCPR response; InitialSizeReceived now requires both size AND cursor
  position responses before returning true
- AnsiOutput: add InlineRowOffset property; apply in SetCursorPositionImpl
- ApplicationImpl.Screen: LayoutAndDraw resizes Screen and sets row offset
  on first inline draw
- ApplicationImpl: add InlineCursorRow internal property
- IterationImpl: pass cursor row from SizeMonitor when inline confirmed
- InlineCLI example: remove AnchorEnd(), view starts at Y=0
- Add 3 new tests for cursor position query behavior

Refactored AppModel and ForceInlineCursorRow to be instance-based properties on IApplication, IDriver, and IComponentFactory, improving testability and multi-app support. Refactor InlineCLI for clarity and style, ensuring output list auto-scrolls and input clears after submission. Move version string out of Logo art and display as a separate View. Adds InlineColorPicker project demonstrating inline ColorPicker usage with new RunnableWrapper<TView, TResult> for dialog-less, typed result extraction. Fixes ApplicationMouse inline coordinate adjustment to prevent double-subtraction on synthesized events. Includes new test for mouse offset correctness and minor ColorPicker code/style improvements. Updates solution file.

* Fix AnsiSizeMonitor startup race; update inline plan doc

Fixes two race conditions in AnsiSizeMonitor: always completes the startup size query even on empty response, and ensures cursor position query completion occurs after parsing the initial row. Updates inline-mode-plan.md with detailed architecture phases and documents these fixes.

* Improve inline mode resize: reset, clear, and redraw

Updated inline mode resize logic to reset the region to row 0, clear the entire terminal, and mark the screen for re-sizing from scratch. This ensures a full redraw and correct layout after terminal resizes by resetting InlineCursorRow, sending a terminal clear sequence, and updating the Screen rectangle.

* Add tests for inline terminal resize behavior

Added InlineResizeTests to verify that after a terminal resize in inline mode, the inline region resets its Y position and width, and the driver's InlineCursorRow is reset to 0. Both tests use the ANSI driver and simulate inline mode scenarios.

* Fix inline mode overflow and add comprehensive tests

Updated ApplicationImpl.Screen.cs to prevent cursor row underflow during overflow adjustment using Math.Max. Added InlineModeTests covering App.Screen positioning, sizing, overflow handling, dynamic growth, resize behavior, output buffer state, mouse event adjustment, and App.Screen vs Driver.Screen independence. All tests follow project conventions and are marked as Copilot-generated.

* Refactor inline mode to use Point InlinePosition

Replaces InlineCursorRow/InlineState with Point InlinePosition (Y=row, X reserved). Updates all APIs, properties, and tests to use the new model. Removes InlineState struct. Improves flexibility and clarifies inline region start for future enhancements.

* Add docs for Inline Mode and usage in Terminal.Gui

Comprehensive documentation for the new Inline Mode (`AppModel.Inline`) in Terminal.Gui, including rendering modes, usage examples, coordinate system, startup sequence, dynamic growth, resize handling, output buffer, clean exit, testing strategies, and the `RunnableWrapper<TView>` utility. Suitable for CLI tools that require output in the terminal scrollback buffer.

* Update Terminal.Gui/App/IApplication.cs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add InlineSelect example and document ANSI inline mode

Added the InlineSelect example project demonstrating inline OptionSelector usage with command-line options and orientation flags. Updated drivers.md to document new ANSI driver inline mode features, including AppModel, InlinePosition, buffer handling, and startup/shutdown behavior. Configured solution and project files accordingly.

* Change AnsiStartupGate type in IApplication interface

The AnsiStartupGate property in IApplication was updated to use the concrete AnsiStartupGate? type instead of the IAnsiStartupGate? interface. This change affects how the property is referenced and assigned throughout the codebase.

* Restore mouse coords before popover event recursion

Restores the original terminal-absolute mouse coordinates to mouseEvent.ScreenPosition before recursively calling RaiseMouseEvent. This ensures correct offset subtraction and accurate event handling below the popover after dismissal.

* Fix inline mode scrolling and mouse offset issues

Refines inline mode to prevent extra scrolling when the view fits exactly at the terminal's bottom row and ensures the shell prompt appears correctly after inline content. Adjusts mouse event handling to avoid double subtraction of inline offsets when dismissing popovers. Adds targeted tests for both cursor positioning and mouse coordinate adjustment. Also sets OptionSelector border style to Rounded.

* Flush Windows console input buffer on exit

Add WindowsVTInputHelper.Flush() using FlushConsoleInputBuffer to clear pending input events, preventing protocol-specific sequences from leaking into the shell. Update AnsiInput to call this method for WindowsVT, ensuring consistent input buffer flushing across platforms.

* Add AI example: Copilot chat & CLI with Terminal.Gui

Added Examples/AI project demonstrating Copilot SDK integration in Terminal.Gui. Includes interactive chat (ChatView), single-turn CLI (SingleTurnView), slash command autocomplete, and model selection. Registered project in solution and added GitHub.Copilot.SDK package.

* Refactor CLI parsing to use System.CommandLine

Replaces manual argument parsing in Program.cs with System.CommandLine, adding improved help, validation, and error handling for --model/-m and prompt arguments. Also updates ChatView border to use both Gradient and Title settings for enhanced UI appearance.

* Refactor ChatView fields, update CLI parsing, clean up code

Refactored unused private fields in ChatView to local variables and made ExitCode a get-only property. Simplified string handling in OnInputAccepted. Updated Program.cs to use HelpAction and improved prompt parsing. In SingleTurnView, condensed Label initialization and streamlined event handling for better readability.

* Upgrade IO.Abstractions packages to 22.1.1

Upgraded System.IO.Abstractions and TestableIO.System.IO.Abstractions.TestingHelpers from 22.1.0 to 22.1.1. Updated Terminal.Gui.2.0.0 .nupkg and .snupkg to reflect these dependency changes.

commit.message:
fix(lobby): remove revalidatePath from startGame, add try/finally to handleStartGame

revalidatePath('/play') inside the startGame Server Action caused Next.js
to re-render the lobby page as part of the action response. The lobby saw
room.status === 'playing', returned null, and fired a server-side
redirect('/play') that raced with the host's direct router.push(), leaving
the spinner stuck indefinitely.

- Remove revalidatePath('/play') from startGame() — the /play landing page
  revalidates naturally on next visit; revalidateMultiplayerPaths() used by
  updateSettings and claimHost is unchanged
- Wrap handleStartGame() in try/catch/finally so setIsStarting(false) always
  runs; unexpected errors are surfaced via setStartError()

as a runner-level env var.

The api-proxy sidecar returns 503 when the real key is absent — a
clear, actionable failure vs a confusing missing-auth error.

- Add GEMINI_API_KEY to the api-proxy env table

- Add GitHub Actions caution note in the required env vars section

- Fix the Limitations section (was incorrectly "only OpenAI/Anthropic")
- Fix the container ports list to include port 10003 (Gemini)
